name: Chin-Up
instructions: Grab a pull‑up bar with an underhand (supinated) grip, hands shoulder‑width apart. Hang with arms fully extended. Pull yourself up by driving your elbows down and back, aiming to bring your chin above the bar. Squeeze your back and biceps at the top. Lower yourself under control to the dead hang position.
safety_notes: Chin‑ups are slightly easier than pull‑ups because the biceps are more involved. If you have elbow pain, try a neutral grip or use rings. Warm up thoroughly before attempting max‑effort sets.
common_mistakes: Not fully extending at the bottom
Using only arms, not engaging lats
